video:video.video:title: Israeli father grieves after three children killed in Iran missile attack
video:video.video:description: The Jewish community rallied round and paid their respects on Thursday to Yitzhak Biton who lost three children after an Iranian missile destroyed their home on Sunday.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 6
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Friday, March 6: More strikes in Iran; Iran fires missiles and drones at Persian Gulf nations; Americans stranded in Middle East begin returning to U.S.; Trump fires Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem.

video:video.video:title: Academy Awards 2026: 'Sinners' 11-month journey to the Oscars
video:video.video:description: Eleven months after its release, Ryan Coogler's "Sinners" heads into the Oscars on March 15 with a record-breaking 16 nominations.

video:video.video:title: Displaced residents of Beirut's southern suburbs comment after Israeli military evacuation notice
video:video.video:description: Thousands of people displaced from Beirut's southern suburbs filled the streets and public squares of the Lebanese capital.

video:video.video:title: Sirens, interceptions over Jerusalem at dawn
video:video.video:description: Sirens sounded and interceptions were seen over Jerusalem in the early hours of Saturday. People headed to bomb shelters across Israel amid the sound of loud booms as Iranian missiles attacked more targets.

video:video.video:title: 'All those suffering from US and Israeli aggression are our strategic partners,' Russia's Lavrov says
video:video.video:description: Russia's Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov said Thursday that "all those suffering from U.S. and Israeli aggression are our strategic partners."

video:video.video:title: Iranian President rejects US demand for an unconditional surrender
video:video.video:description: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian also apologized on Saturday for Iran's attacks on neighbouring countries, suggesting it was the result of miscommunication in the ranks and insisted that it would stop from now on.

video:video.video:title: The hopes and fears of London's Iranian community
video:video.video:description: Iranian communities abroad are closely watching events unfolding in their homeland after U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iranian targets triggered retaliatory missile and drone attacks by Tehran.

video:video.video:title: Some Palestinians see little hope for reconstruction in Gaza amid intensifying war in Middle East
video:video.video:description: The United Nations estimates the entire enclave has more than 61 million tons of debris. Meanwhile Palestinians continue to live in tents amid the rubble, without access to infrastructure or facilities.

video:video.video:title: A Connecticut fire department is rolling out a new dispatch system that aims to ease stress
video:video.video:description: Danbury, Connecticut has joined hundreds of other US cities that have installed new firehouse alarm systems aimed at reducing firefighters' stress when calls come in.

video:video.video:title: Brash, bare-knuckle-fighting Sherlock Holmes hits small screens
video:video.video:description: Hero Fiennes Tiffin plays a young Sherlock Holmes in Guy Ritchie's rip-roaring, explosive reimaging of Arthur Conan Doyle's fictional sleuth - with added fight scenes.

video:video.video:title: Thousands flee Beirut suburbs after Israeli order signals further strikes
video:video.video:description: Thousands fled from the southern suburbs of Beirut on Thursday after the Israeli army warned all residents of the densely populated area to evacuate ahead of launching new wave of strikes.

video:video.video:title: Sri Lanka navy releases video showing rescue of Iranian sailors from warship off coast
video:video.video:description: Sri Lanka has released video showing its navy personnel rescuing Iranian sailors from a warship that was sunk by a torpedo from a U.S. submarine on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Interception trails visible in Tel Aviv area following Iranian missile attack
video:video.video:description: Sirens sounded again in Tel Aviv, Israel on Friday following launches from Iran. Trails from interceptions were visible in the sky, and the sound of explosions could be heard

video:video.video:title: Winter Paralympics begins for its 50th anniversary
video:video.video:description: The Winter Paralympics are in Milan Cortina to celebrate their 50th anniversary, with China looking to extend its dominance as a Paralympic powerhouse and Ukraine and other nations boycotting the opening ceremony over the return of the Russian flag and anthem.

video:video.video:title: Israeli military says video shows strike on Khamenei's bunker in Tehran days after his death
video:video.video:description: The Israeli military released a video that it said showed a strike on a bunker that allegedly belonged to Iran’s Supreme Leader, Ali Khamenei on Friday, days after his death.

video:video.video:title: Ohtani puts on home run show before hitting a grand slam at the World Baseball Classic
video:video.video:description: Japanese baseball star Shohei Ohtani thrilled a packed crowd on Friday, launching ball after ball over the fence during batting practice before hitting a grand slam at the World Baseball Classic.

video:video.video:title: Britney Spears arrested for DUI
video:video.video:description: Authorities say Britney Spears was pulled over, after reports that her BMW was driving fast and erratically, on a highway Wednesday night and jailed after taking a series of field sobriety tests.

video:video.video:title: Sam Claflin starstruck by Ben Whishaw
video:video.video:description: Ever sat opposite a celebrity on the train? Sam Claflin has and - to the delight of his "Vanished" co-star Kaley Cuoco - he describes the moment he spotted "Paddington" star Ben Whishaw.

video:video.video:title: Issey Miyake's quiet collection
video:video.video:description: Sculptured head pieces, cloud-like puff ball skirts and asymmetric suits, at Issey Miyake's Paris show Friday.

video:video.video:title: Strong storms in Oklahoma as millions are under tornado watch
video:video.video:description: A powerful storm in Oklahoma is being investigated as a possible tornado as millions of Americans are under threat from severe weather.

video:video.video:title: Jobs report reveals unexpected 92,000 job cuts as unemployment rises
video:video.video:description: U.S. employers cut 92,000 jobs last month and pushed unemployment up to 4.4% in what economist Diane Swonk calls a “jobless boom.” (AP Video: Ted Shaffrey)

video:video.video:title: For survivors, Rhode Island clergy abuse report brings vindication
video:video.video:description: A report by Rhode Island’s attorney general detailing decades of sexual abuse by Catholic clergy is giving survivors a sense of vindication while renewing calls for accountability and support.

video:video.video:title: UN chief warns that the war in the Middle East 'could spiral beyond anyone's control'
video:video.video:description: U.N. Secretary-General António Guterres called for an end to the war in the Middle East that now affects more than a dozen countries, and for serious diplomatic negotiations, warning the conflict could spiral out of control.

video:video.video:title: Ex-Michigan football coach Sherrone Moore reaches plea deal to resolve criminal case
video:video.video:description: Former Michigan football coach Sherrone Moore pleaded no contest to two misdemeanors in a criminal case (AP video: Mike Householder and Larry Lage)

video:video.video:title: What to know before using AI chatbots for health advice
video:video.video:description: With hundreds of millions of people turning to chatbots for advice, it was only a matter of time before tech companies began offering programs designed specifically to answer health questions.

video:video.video:title: A chance to own Kurt Cobain's 'Smells Like Teen Spirit' guitar or John Lennon's piano
video:video.video:description: Christie's auction house in New York is set to have one of the most extensive Rock n Roll and pop culture auctions in recent history next week.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 6
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, March 6th: Evidence suggests deadly airstrike on Iranian school likely from U.S.; Donald Trump wants "unconditional surrender" of Iran; U.S. unemployment rate rises to 4.4% and former presidents attend Rev.

video:video.video:title: Debris sent flying as suspected tornado strikes southwest Michigan
video:video.video:description: A suspected tornado hit Three Rivers, Michigan on Friday, as powerful storms ripped across the state.

video:video.video:title: Seth MacFarlane explains why he used AI in ‘Ted’
video:video.video:description: Seth MacFarlane explains how AI helped him achieve one goal in his TV series "Ted": looking like Bill Clinton.

video:video.video:title: Trump hosts White House roundtable aimed at 'saving college sports'
video:video.video:description: Last year, President Donald Trump endorsed the the Score Act, which would set new rules for the payment of college athletes and set nationwide standards for compensation, but the act has stalled in Congress.

video:video.video:title: Explosion and plumes of smoke rising in Beirut following Israeli strike
video:video.video:description: An explosion was heard and large plumes of smoke were seen rising in Beirut's southern suburbs on Friday after Israeli airstrikes pounded the capitals of Iran and Lebanon as the U.S. apparently struck an Iranian drone carrier at sea, intensifying its campaign targeting the Islamic Republic’s fleet o

video:video.video:title: BLACKPINK's Jisoo stars in 'Boyfriend on Demand'
video:video.video:description: BLACKPINK member Jisoo says her new Netflix series "Boyfriend on Demand," which drops today, taps into the very real and modern life anxieties of navigating dating and finding love — and she hopes viewers see themselves in her character.

video:video.video:title: Satoshi Kondo's sculptures at Issey Miyake
video:video.video:description: Satoshi Kondo unveiled a collection featuring silhouettes, dark materials and bright colors at the Issey Miyake Fall/Winter 2026 Paris fashion show on Friday. (March 6.)

video:video.video:title: AP reports: Israel reinforces northern border with Lebanon but yet to evacuate villages
video:video.video:description: Israeli forces are reinforcing communities along the northern border as missiles and drones continue to be launched from Lebanon, according to The Associated Press's Sam Mednick.

video:video.video:title: UN sees growing humanitarian crisis in Middle East as conflicts spread
video:video.video:description: The United Nations says the humanitarian situation in the Middle East is a moment of grave peril, with crises escalating rapidly and consequences that are out of control .

video:video.video:title: Cat rescued after stuck in tree for 3 days
video:video.video:description: After three days of cries for help, a stranded cat was finally rescued. Video shows professional climber Steven Murrow scaling a tall tree Sunday to reach the feline.

video:video.video:title: Bail set at $2M for Cleveland woman accused of killing her 2 daughters
video:video.video:description: A Cleveland judge set bail at $2 million Friday for a woman accused of killing her two young daughters and burying them in suitcases in shallow graves.

video:video.video:title: Israel reinforces northern border with Lebanon but has yet to evacuate villages
video:video.video:description: Israeli forces reinforce northern border communities as missiles and drones launch from Lebanon, AP’s Sam Mednick reports.

video:video.video:title: Oil surges to its highest price since 2023, and stocks drop after data shows a weaker US job market
video:video.video:description: The combination of a weak economy and high inflation is a worst-case scenario for investors because the Federal Reserve has no good tool to fix both problems at the same time.

video:video.video:title: Israeli warplanes bomb Tehran as war in the region escalates
video:video.video:description: Israeli warplanes bombed Tehran early on Friday while U.S. President appeared to rule out talks with Iran in a social media post calling for its “unconditional surrender.”

video:video.video:title: 'We are living in a time when it can be hard to hope,' Obama swipes at Trump during Jackson eulogy
video:video.video:description: Former President Barack Obama took a swipe at President Donald Trump, without mentioning him by name, at the final public tribute to the late civil rights leader, the Rev.

video:video.video:title: J Balvin jumps at chance to record FIFA World Cup anthem
video:video.video:description: J Balvin helped reimagine Van Halen’s "Jump" for Coca-Cola’s official anthem for the FIFA World Cup 2026.

video:video.video:title: Former presidents and former vice president attend Rev. Jesse Jackson's funeral
video:video.video:description: Former Presidents Barack Obama, Bill Clinton and Joe Biden, former Vice President Kamala Harris and former first ladies Jill Biden and Hillary Clinton attended a final public tribute for the late Rev.

video:video.video:title: Kamala Harris: 'I predicted a lot about what's happening'
video:video.video:description: Former U.S. Vice President Kamala Harris shared a message about breaking doors down at Rev. Jesse Jackson's funeral.

video:video.video:title: Former President Barack Obama reflects on the life of Rev. Jesse Jackson
video:video.video:description: Former President Barack Obama shares details about his last visit with the late Rev. Jesse Jackson. "He starts coming up with this project and this initiative."

video:video.video:title: Drones target oilfields in southern Iraq
video:video.video:description: At least two drones struck energy infrastructure and facilities of U.S. contractors on Friday evening, according to two security officials who spoke on the condition of anonymity as they were not authorized to speak with the press.

video:video.video:title: Minneapolis activists, business owners and residents reflect on Kristi Noem’s departure
video:video.video:description: Minneapolis activists, business owners and residents reflect on Kristi Noem’s departure as Homeland Security secretary and explain how an immigration crackdown in Minnesota changed their lives.

video:video.video:title: Pentagon vs. Anthropic: How an AI dispute turned into a national security clash
video:video.video:description: The Pentagon has labeled AI company Anthropic a supply chain risk after a clash with the Trump administration over the military's use of artificial intelligence.

video:video.video:title: Israeli warplanes bomb Tehran
video:video.video:description: Israeli warplanes bombed Tehran early on Friday and again on Saturday. Meanwhile, U.S. President Donald Trump appeared to rule out talks with Iran in a social media post calling for its “unconditional surrender.”

video:video.video:title: Ryan Gosling reveals his 'harshest critics'
video:video.video:description: Ryan Gosling says his daughters inspired one of his wardrobe choices in "Project Hail Mary." He plays a science teacher who journeys to space to save the world in the sci-fi film.

video:video.video:title: Jennifer Hudson performs at Rev. Jesse Jackson's funeral
video:video.video:description: Jennifer Hudson performed Sam Cooke's "A Change is Gonna Come" at Rev. Jesse Jackson's funeral. The song is considered an anthem of the Civil Rights Movement.

video:video.video:title: Kamala Harris eulogizes Jackson as a leader who fought barriers to 'opportunity, justice and freedom'
video:video.video:description: Kamala Harris eulogizes Jackson as a leader who fought barriers to 'opportunity, justice and freedom'

video:video.video:title: Apparent Iran ballistic missile over occupied West Bank drops what expert says are cluster munitions
video:video.video:description: A video taken by AP shows what appear to be Iranian ballistic missiles flying over the occupied West Bank and dropping smaller bomblets, which are found inside the missile.

video:video.video:title: Trump rules out talks absent Iran's 'unconditional surrender' as Israel strikes Lebanon
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump appeared to rule out talks with Iran absent its “unconditional surrender.”

video:video.video:title: Iranian worshipers attend Friday prayers, shout defiant chants at rally despite continued strikes
video:video.video:description: Worshipers attended a rally after Friday prayers in Tehran, as the Iranian capital continues to be pounded by airstrikes, and Iran said it had fired ballistic missiles toward Israel.

video:video.video:title: Iran war raises worries in the US over higher gas prices
video:video.video:description: Oil surged higher and showed no signs of halting its rapid climb a week after the U.S. and Israel launched major attacks on Iran that escalated into a war in the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: Travel insiders give advice amidst Iran war travel chaos
video:video.video:description: The Iran war continues to upended travel across the Middle East and beyond. After almost a week of conflict, airspace closures and flight cancelations, travel industry insiders recommend postponing unnecessary travel if possible, checking refund or insurance policies and, most importantly, monitorin

video:video.video:title: Renaissance master Michelangelo as revered as ever centuries on
video:video.video:description: The Italian sculptor and painter Michelangelo's genius and legacy are as strong as ever 500 years after his death.

video:video.video:title: Smoke seen rising from warehouses near Irbil airport in Iraq's northern Kurdish region
video:video.video:description: Smoke from a suspected drone attack was seen rising from warehouses belonging to various international oil and construction companies near Irbil International Airport in Iraq on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Women in South Africa take up guns and martial arts for protection against gender violence
video:video.video:description: Data by the Human Sciences Research Council shows that more than 35% of women over the age of 18 in South Africa have experienced physical or sexual violence, while the country's femicide rates are said to be five times the global average.

video:video.video:title: Chappell Roan, Paris Jackson attend Vivienne Westwood show
video:video.video:description: Andreas Kronthaler presented his latest collection for Vivienne Westwood in Paris, inspired by the works of Italian costume designer Danilo Donati and actress Romy Schneider.

video:video.video:title: More airstrikes hit Tehran
video:video.video:description: More airstrikes hit Iran's capital of Tehran as night falls on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Explosions and flames seen in southern Tehran following new airstrikes
video:video.video:description: Several airstrikes hit neighborhoods in Tehran’s east and southwest Saturday night as Israel’s military confirmed a new wave of attacks. They came after U.S.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian soldiers commemorate commander Dmytro "Da Vinci" Kotsiubailo during oath-taking ceremony
video:video.video:description: Dmytro Kotsiubailo, callsign Da Vinci, was killed in action three years ago. Soldiers gathered on the grave of Kotsiubailo in Kyiv, singing the national anthem during an oath-taking ceremony for new recruits.

video:video.video:title: Iranian president on demand of unconditional surrender: 'They should take that dream to their grave'
video:video.video:description: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian apologized on Saturday for Iran's attacks on neighboring countries, suggesting it was the result of miscommunication in the ranks and insisted that it would stop from now on.

video:video.video:title: Explosions light up the sky following airstrikes on western Tehran
video:video.video:description: Several explosions lit up the sky in western Tehran as Israeli warplanes pounded the Iranian capital in the early hours of Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Footage captures a powerful tornado in Michigan devastating homes in its path
video:video.video:description: At Lisa Piper's home near Union City, Michigan, she can be heard repeatedly yelling out, as she films from her back deck a ferocious rotating column of air that appears to be a tornado tear through a section of buildings across the lake from her.

video:video.video:title: Chinese company showcases largest electrical aircraft, but analyst says flying taxis are years off
video:video.video:description: A Chinese company has developed what it claims is the world’s largest flying electrical vertical take-off and landing vehicle, or eVTOL.

video:video.video:title: 23 dead, flights disrupted and military deployed after heavy rains pound Kenya's capital overnight
video:video.video:description: Twenty-three people died, flights were disrupted and the military was deployed as motorists in the Kenyan capital were stranded for hours on Saturday after roads were flooded following heavy rains overnight.

video:video.video:title: UK's chief of defence staff on deployments to the Middle East and UK's preparedness
video:video.video:description: The British military deployed four additional Royal Air Force (RAF) Typhoon fighter jets to Qatar on Saturday as the conflict escalates.

video:video.video:title: Trump says US will turn attention to Cuba after war with Iran
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump said "great change" will soon be coming to Cuba and hinted his administration might cut a deal with Havana.

video:video.video:title: 'This had to be done': Trump claims to have done the world a 'favor' with attacks on Iran
video:video.video:description: It comes as Iran's president apologized on Saturday for attacks on “neighboring countries” even as its missiles and drones flew toward Gulf Arab states and Trump threatened that the country would be “hit very hard.”

video:video.video:title: Lebanese village wakes up to destruction after 16 killed in night of Israeli airstrikes
video:video.video:description: Residents of a town in Lebanon’s eastern Bekaa Valley walked among the rubble and looked in disbelief Saturday at the vast destruction from overnight clashes that left 16 dead and 35 wounded, according to Lebanon's Health Ministry.

video:video.video:title: Nightclub bombing in Peru injures 33, including minors, authorities say
video:video.video:description: A bombing at a nightclub in Peru has injured 33 people, including minors, authorities said Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Jan. 6 plaque honoring police officers is installed at the Capitol after a 3-year delay
video:video.video:description: Visitors to the Capitol in Washington now have a visible reminder of the violent attack against the building on Jan. 6, 2021, and the officers who fought and were injured that day.

video:video.video:title: Trump joins grieving families for the return of soldiers killed in war in the Middle East
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump on Saturday joined grieving families at Dover Air Force Base at the dignified transfer for the six U.S. soldiers killed in the war in the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: At least 7 killed in Ukraine's Kharkiv after Russian missile hits apartment building
video:video.video:description: Russia has fired tens of thousands of Iranian-designed Shahed drones at Ukraine since it invaded its neighbor just over four years ago.

video:video.video:title: After a president-filled celebration, Rev. Jesse Jackson's family gathers for an intimate homegoing
video:video.video:description: A day after former presidents, sitting governors and local Chicago residents alike attended a vibrant, televised celebration for the late Rev.

video:video.video:title: Residents of Michigan community come together in wake of deadly storm
video:video.video:description: Residents of a Michigan community are coming together to pick up the pieces after a deadly storm passed through. (AP video: Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 7
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Saturday, March 7th: Trump encourages Latin American leaders to use military action against cartels; Iranian President apologizes to Gulf Arab states: Oil and gas prices rapidly rise as Iran war continues; Severe weather kill at least six in the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Trump encourages Latin American leaders to use military action to help US fight cartels
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Saturday that the U.S. and Latin American countries were banding together to combat violent cartels.

video:video.video:title: Trump downplays reports that Russia is providing Iran information on US targets in the Middle East
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said it's inconsequential if Russia has provided Iran with information to help Tehran target U.S. military personnel and assets in the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: Hometown Halfpipe Hero: Eileen Gu leads San Francisco’s Chinese New Year Parade
video:video.video:description: Olympic freestyle skiing champion Eileen Gu returned to her hometown of San Francisco as Grand Marshal of the San Francisco Chinese New Year Parade, receiving a warm welcome from crowds despite past controversy over her decision to compete for China in the Olympics instead of the United States.

video:video.video:title: Israel claims to have struck Iran airport it says was used to support militants in Middle East
video:video.video:description: The Israeli military on Saturday reported that it had struck Mehrabad Airport in Tehran that it alleged was used to transport weapons to militant groups that Iran supports across the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: Black smoke billows into Beirut sky after explosion as Israel continues to launch strikes
video:video.video:description: Israel renewed its assault on southern Lebanon early Sunday, including targeting commanders of the Lebanese branch of Iran’s Revolutionary Guards’ Quds Force, after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu promised “many surprises” for the next phase of the conflict.

video:video.video:title: Fires send huge clouds of smoke into Tehran night sky after barrage of Israeli airstrikes
video:video.video:description: Pillars of flame rose above an oil storage facility in Tehran late on Saturday as Israel carried out further air strikes.

video:video.video:title: From needlepoint to mahjong, young people are embracing ‘grandma hobbies’
video:video.video:description: Droves of young people are turning to tactile, analog hobbies and activities, such as needlework and mahjong, as a means of escaping technology and reconnecting with creativity and exploration.

video:video.video:title: Top Iranian security official insists 'no divisions' in Tehran leadership over war with U.S. and Israel
video:video.video:description: Top Iranian security official Ali Larijani has asserted that "our leaders are united... and have no disagreements with one another".
